req.title: Message to provider
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-025 requires that authenticated patients can send messages to providers, with automatic PHI exfiltration scanning on attachments. The requirement has three critical paths: (1) happy path—message with safe attachment succeeds; (2) security path—message with disallowed PHI pattern is blocked; (3) boundary path—edge cases around attachment size, file type, and pattern detection. Equivalence partitioning separates safe vs. unsafe attachments. Boundary value analysis covers attachment size limits. Negative path testing validates rejection of PHI patterns. Decision table logic applies to attachment validation rules.

tests.name: test_message_to_provider_with_safe_attachment_succeeds
preconditions: patient authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: POST /messages with text body and PDF attachment (clean content)
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_message_to_provider_with_safe_attachment_succeeds():
    auth_token = '<jwt_token>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    payload = {
        'provider_id': 'prov-001',
        'subject': 'Test Message',
        'body': 'Patient consultation notes.'
    }
    files = {'attachment': ('report.pdf', b'PDF_BINARY_DATA', 'application/pdf')}
    resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/messages',
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        files=files
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'message_id' in data
    assert data['status'] == 'stored'
    assert data['attachment']['scan_result'] == 'clean'
    assert 'provider_notification' in data

tests.name: test_message_blocked_when_ssn_pattern_detected_in_attachment
preconditions: patient authenticated with valid JWT token
PHI pattern regex for SSN (\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}) configured
actions: POST /messages with text body and TXT attachment containing SSN
attachment passes initial file type validation
trace: R-HLT-025: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
Security: PHI pattern match on SSN
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_message_blocked_when_ssn_pattern_detected_in_attachment():
    auth_token = '<jwt_token>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    payload = {
        'provider_id': 'prov-001',
        'subject': 'Test Message',
        'body': 'Forwarding patient info.'
    }
    malicious_content = b'SSN: 123-45-6789\nDOB: 1990-01-15'
    files = {'attachment': ('data.txt', malicious_content, 'text/plain')}
    resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/messages',
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        files=files
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error']['code'] == 'PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED'
    assert 'SSN' in data['error']['detail']

tests.name: test_message_boundary_max_attachment_size
preconditions: patient authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: attachment content is random binary (no PHI patterns)
tests.executable:
import requests
import io

def test_message_boundary_max_attachment_size():
    auth_token = '<jwt_token>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    max_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024
    binary_payload = b'X' * max_size
    payload = {
        'provider_id': 'prov-001',
        'subject': 'Large File',
        'body': 'Sending large attachment.'
    }
    files = {'attachment': ('large.bin', io.BytesIO(binary_payload), 'application/octet-stream')}
    resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/messages',
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        files=files
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['status'] == 'stored'
    assert data['attachment']['size'] == max_size

tests.name: test_message_rejected_when_attachment_exceeds_max_size
preconditions: patient authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: attachment content is random binary (no PHI patterns)
tests.executable:
import requests
import io

def test_message_rejected_when_attachment_exceeds_max_size():
    auth_token = '<jwt_token>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    max_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1
    binary_payload = b'X' * max_size
    payload = {
        'provider_id': 'prov-001',
        'subject': 'Large File',
        'body': 'Oversized.'
    }
    files = {'attachment': ('large.bin', io.BytesIO(binary_payload), 'application/octet-stream')}
    resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/messages',
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        files=files
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error']['code'] == 'ATTACHMENT_SIZE_EXCEEDED'

tests.name: test_message_blocked_when_medical_record_number_detected
preconditions: patient authenticated with valid JWT token
PHI pattern regex for MRN configured
actions: POST /messages with attachment containing MRN pattern
trace: R-HLT-025: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
Security: PHI pattern match on MRN
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_message_blocked_when_medical_record_number_detected():
    auth_token = '<jwt_token>'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    payload = {
        'provider_id': 'prov-001',
        'subject': 'Patient Data',
        'body': 'See attached.'
    }
    csv_content = b'patient_id,name,mrn\n1,John Doe,MRN#ABC123456\n'
    files = {'attachment': ('patients.csv', csv_content, 'text/csv')}
    resp = requests.post(
        'http://localhost:8080/messages',
        headers=headers,
        data=payload,
        files=files
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error']['code'] == 'PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED'
    assert 'MRN' in data['error']['detail']
